claim: OpenAI's Agent Builder is a no-code visual workflow editor for building agents; it uses node-based logic similar to n8n and Make.com
actionable: Use Agent Builder for rapid prototyping of multi-step agent workflows; design classification, branching, and tool-calling logic visually before exporting code.

claim: Agent Builder includes guardrails for jailbreak detection, harmful content moderation, and hallucination grounding via vector stores
actionable: Enable jailbreak guardrails early in workflows; ground responses with file search to reduce hallucinations in domain-specific tasks.

claim: You can structure agent outputs as JSON enums for classification; use if-else conditional nodes to route based on classification results
actionable: Define structured output schemas with enum fields for multi-way classification; route execution paths based on the classified field value.

claim: Agent Builder supports file search for building mini-RAG systems; upload documents and let agents retrieve relevant excerpts per query
actionable: Upload domain-specific documents as vector stores in Agent Builder; agents will search and cite relevant passages for grounded responses.

claim: Agent Builder lets you add notes to workflows for brainstorming; you can describe a skill and have it auto-generate implementation details
actionable: Use notes to draft agent behavior before implementing nodes; leverage the builder's auto-generation features to scaffold complex workflows quickly.

claim: You can export Agent Builder workflows as AgentKit SDK code and customize models, file search, and guardrails in the exported code
actionable: Build workflows visually in Agent Builder, then export and extend with AgentKit SDK code for production customization.
